annotation.text: This isn’t quite right. Published means it has been published in a public way—live on a website, printed to a physical volume intended for public distribution. It’s an indication that this version is in a terminal stage and can’t be rescinded. It can be deleted, but not changed. Itshould have the same meaning and contract as publication to the npm registry. If you want to make changes to a published item, you have to create a new version of the item and induct it into a non-terminal pipeline stage. There’s no connection between the terminal published state and its disposition in the filesystem or conceptual content collection.
annotation.anchor: the file gets pushed to the destination collection
